fix_summary: Check vacuum line to MAP sensor, inspect intake manifold seals (common HD leak point), test MAP voltage.

description: Intake Air Temperature Sensor Low Input
common_causes: Faulty IAT sensor in air cleaner
fix_summary: Inspect IAT sensor in air cleaner housing, check for water in connector (common in rain riding).

description: Engine Coolant Temperature Sensor Low Input
fix_summary: Test ECT sensor resistance (check HD service manual for spec at temp), inspect connector at cylinder head.

description: Throttle Position Sensor Circuit Low Input
fix_summary: Check TPS base voltage (should be ~0.5V at closed throttle), adjust or replace. HD TPS is adjustable on many models.
Check for exhaust leaks at header-to-head joint (very common on HD), inspect O2 sensor, check fuel trim.
Check fuel pressure regulator, inspect injectors for leaking, verify no oil in intake (breather issue).
Check injector connector, measure injector resistance (should be ~12 ohms), swap injectors to isolate.
Measure injector resistance, check wiring for shorts, swap injectors between cylinders to isolate.

description: Evaporative Emission System Purge Control Valve Circuit Open
fix_summary: Check purge valve connector and vacuum lines. Common on touring models.
Check VSS on transmission case, inspect tone ring through sensor hole, check wiring to ECM.
Test stator AC output (should be ~18-22V AC per leg at 2000 RPM), check regulator, clean battery terminals. HD stators are a known failure point.
Check main relay under seat, test relay coil resistance, inspect fuse box for corrosion.
Check coil primary resistance (~2-3 ohms) and secondary (~10-15K ohms), inspect connector for heat damage.
Same as P1002 but for rear cylinder coil. HD coils sit close to engine — heat damage is common.
Spray carb cleaner around intake seals while running — RPM change indicates leak. Replace seals (very common HD issue).
Check spark, fuel injector pulse, compression. If P1002 also present, likely coil failure.
Check spark, fuel injector pulse, compression. If P1003 also present, likely coil failure.
This is a secondary code — check for primary codes that triggered limp mode. Fix primary issue first.

description: Loss of ECM Serial Data Communication
fix_summary: Check CAN bus wiring (twisted pair), inspect ECM connector for corrosion, verify ground connections.
Check fuel sender resistance with tank at known levels, inspect connector on top of fuel tank.
